---
title: 'IN JESUS NAME, AMEN?'
date: '2021-05-27T16:55:00.0010000+00:00'
draft: false
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '4176673209431699205'
blogger_filename: '/2021/05/in-jesus-name-amen.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Musings'
---

<div>IN JESUS NAME, AMEN?</div><div><br></div><div>While this doesn't "bother" me when I hear it, I have tended to stop using his name to end prayers. I will address him by name, as I would anyone else I was speaking with.</div><div><br></div><div>"Hey Jen, let's go for a walk..."</div><div><br></div><div>"Hey Jesus, let's talk about this...."</div><div><br></div><div>"... Jesus, thank you so much for your love and affection for us."</div><div><br></div><div>But the phrase "in my name" wasn't meant to be a sign-off or a tagline that you add to a request. When we use it this way, it obscures and dilutes its original and intended meaning.</div><div><br></div><div>It's a turn of phrase.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>It has a long history going back to Genesis One (Image Bearer). Israel bore then name of YHWH. We bear the name of YHWH Incarnate, Jesus.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>When we operate in the authority of Jesus, bearing the name of YHWH Incarnate Jesus, on the mission for Jesus, doing His work....... Ask what ye will....&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>If I am where He told me to be, doing what he told me to do, then I am an authorized user on my Father's business credit card account.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>If I am not where he told me to be, doing what he told me to do, my authority is revoked.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>So no, I cannot simply wish for a Mercedes and call it in JUST because I want to.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>You have not because you don't ask, or, when you do ask you ask with selfish motives.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>In other words, if I am listening to the Holy Spirit, and doing what he told me to do, I can ask for ANYTHING He tells me to ask for, and it'll be done.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>If I'm not on mission, it doesn't apply.</div><div><br></div><div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;">
  <a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1622134516960315-0-fadc50cf47e1.png" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;">
    <img border="0" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1622134516960315-0-fadc50cf47e1.png" width="400">
  </a>
</div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;">
  <a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1622134511459772-1-dadfe0faa983.png" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;">
    <img border="0" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1622134511459772-1-dadfe0faa983.png" width="400">
  </a>
</div><br></div>
